assessment.summary: Two independent, ordered implementation tracks. Track A (issue hierarchy) is a prerequisite for Track B (ephemeral execution) because the ephemeral model targets IMPLEMENT parents, not ANALYZE issues.
key_gaps: polaris-analyze step 05 does not create a separate IMPLEMENT parent issue; clusters.json source_id points to ANALYZE issue
polaris-run has no guard against being pointed at an ANALYZE parent
AgentSubtaskAdapter.dispatch() is a stub — throws — blocking native Claude ephemeral dispatch
orchestration_mode in runtime-state.ts is present but not semantically activated
No MCP claim/dispatch-result/spawn operations exist for ephemeral mode
evidence: src/loop/adapters/agent-subtask.ts: dispatch() throws unconditionally — no implementation
src/types/runtime-state.ts: orchestration_mode and runtime_generation fields present but unused in parent.ts
.polaris/clusters/POL-97/clusters.json: source_id = POL-97 (the ANALYZE issue), not a separate IMPLEMENT parent
src/loop/parent.ts: isAnalyzeChild() checks child titles, not whether the cluster root is an ANALYZE issue
.codex/skills/polaris-run/chain.md: no step guards against ANALYZE parent as execution target

notes: This run is itself the first demonstration of the new ANALYZE→IMPLEMENT parent model: POL-104 is the ANALYZE issue, POL-105 is the IMPLEMENT parent, clusters.json source_id points to POL-105.
